front: How does 'count' work in Terraform and when should you use it?
back:
count creates multiple instances of a resource from a single block. Use it when you need N identical (or nearly identical) copies of a resource.

Basic count:
```hcl
resource "aws_s3_bucket" "data_zones" {
  count  = 3
  bucket = "mycompany-zone-${count.index}"
}
# Creates: mycompany-zone-0, mycompany-zone-1, mycompany-zone-2
```

Count with a list variable (common pattern):
```hcl
variable "data_zones" {
  default = ["raw", "curated", "processed"]
}

resource "aws_s3_bucket" "zones" {
  count  = length(var.data_zones)
  bucket = "mycompany-${var.data_zones[count.index]}"
}
# Creates: mycompany-raw, mycompany-curated, mycompany-processed
```

Conditional resource (count = 0 or 1):
```hcl
resource "aws_cloudwatch_alarm" "high_cost" {
  count = var.environment == "prod" ? 1 : 0
  # Only create cost alarm in prod
}
```

Limitation of count: resources are addressed by INDEX (aws_s3_bucket.zones[0]). If you remove an item from the middle of the list, Terraform shifts all higher indexes and may destroy/recreate many resources. Use for_each with a set/map for stable identities.
